site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 8 wrap board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 8 review notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: round 8 landed as a perfect 8 from 8 on the tipping card
no charity bet was placed in round 8 so the mark hughes foundation bank stays at $6.50
discipline mattered more than action and the board avoided forcing a short price
the tigers round 7 miss still sits in the ledger but round 8 steadied the process nicely
round 9 strategy should wait for fresh team lists before the board starts leaning again
today is for logging the result cleanly rather than pretending we already know next week

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: Bulldogs head to head leads the early shortlist
The board stayed patient and did not force a Round 8 charity play
That restraint mattered because the bank stayed protected at $6.50
Perfect tipping matters too, and 8 from 8 is worth banking mentally
Round 9 should begin only after fresh team lists arrive

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: The Tigers charity miss is still the last bet in the ledger, so emotion can creep in if we rush the next play
A perfect tipping round can create false confidence if we start guessing ahead of team lists
Round 9 prices and injury edges will move quickly once lists drop
The main risk now is updating too early and building the next board on stale information
Keep the process patient for another day

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Log the Round 8 win cleanly and use it as proof the process still works
Carry the MH Foundation bank unchanged into Round 9
Wait for Team List Tuesday before deciding what deserves real attention
Separate comp tipping success from charity betting discipline
Stay selective on the next bet after the Tigers loss

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Any market overreaction after the perfect 8 from 8 week
Whether the next charity play comes from a short-priced favourite or a value dog
How much trust to put in momentum versus updated lists

betCard.title: Round 8 Charity Bet
why: The board did not see enough value to force a play after the Tigers miss in Round 7.
That discipline protected the remaining charity bank at $6.50.
A no-bet week is a valid outcome when price and confidence do not line up.
risks: The only real risk was missing a winner, but that is cheaper than forcing a weak number.
Waiting too long to reset emotionally after a loss can also become a risk if it turns into hesitation.
Round 9 still needs fresh evaluation, not a carry-over assumption from Round 8.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 8 closes with the process back on track
lines: As of Monday, 28 April 2026, the Round 8 card has landed as a perfect 8 from 8 on the tipping side.
No charity bet was placed this week, so the Mark Hughes Foundation bank stays at $6.50 with a 2-1 record.
That is a good outcome: strong comp tipping, no forced play, and a clean reset into Round 9.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: No-bet discipline was the right Round 8 call
lines: After the Tigers charity miss in Round 7, the board did not force a Round 8 recovery bet just to feel active.
That restraint preserved the bank while still allowing the tipping process to produce a perfect week.
The lesson is simple: passing is a win when the market does not offer a clean charity edge.
Round 9 can now be assessed from a calmer position once official team lists arrive.

Sea Eagles over Eels

sections.title: Next Step
sections.heading: Best workflow before the next board refresh
lines: Lock the Round 8 wrap into the board now.
Carry the charity ledger forward unchanged.
Wait for Round 9 team lists before rebuilding leans.
Do not front-run injury or suspension news without official confirmation.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: What last week taught us
lines: Hot: the tipping process itself, because it stayed clean and landed 8 from 8.
Cold: emotional betting after a loss, because the no-bet decision was the right correction.
The biggest upgrade is confidence in the workflow, not blind confidence in any one team.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: What to wait for before touching Round 9
lines: Official Round 9 team lists are the next real trigger for updating strategy.
Fresh prices matter more than recycled Round 8 logic once a new slate opens.
Any new injury or suspension fallout should be logged before forming leans.
The next charity shortlist should only be built once both lists and markets are visible together.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
